description: HONEST SCOPE: an INSTANTANEOUS STATUS PROXY, not ISO-22400 availability (that would be a runtime/planned-time RATIO — the time-ratio lane is historians/postgresql-cagg/oee-hourly.json, with its own documented hardcodes). Maps the machine's current status to 100 (producing) or 0 (not producing); anything outside the measured vocabulary yields null, because an unknown status is an UNANSWERED QUESTION, not zero availability. Vocabulary: Act_Status_Machine was measured 2026-07-12 over the full population (22.1M rows) = {RUNNING, IDLE, STOPPED, MAINTENANCE}. v1 additionally matched 'PRODUCING'/'RUN'/'PROD', numeric codes 1/2/3 (a numeric literal against a String attribute can never compare equal), and 'WARNING'/'SETUP'/'STANDBY' at 50% — NONE of these values has ever occurred; every one of those arms was dead. The 50%-tier is gone with them: it graded fictional states. APPLIES TO CNC ONLY: the IMM profile's `status` vocabulary is NOT MEASURED yet and its profile explicitly forbids guards on it — extending this KPI to the IMM requires measuring that vocabulary first (no invented enums), then adding an inputMappings entry; ci/lint-kpis.mjs refuses guards on enum-less attributes. The input keeps the real wire name Act_Status_Machine, so the consumer's exact-name matcher binds it TODAY without the explicit map (simulated 2026-07-15); inputMappings carries the identity binding for the explicit KpiFlowOptions.inputMap contract. NOTE: the exporter does not yet enforce appliesTo on push — pushing this KPI to an IMM would let the unmeasured `status` fuzzy-match the input; that enforcement is a consumer-side follow-up.

calculation.cypher: CASE WHEN m.Act_Status_Machine = 'RUNNING' THEN 100.0 WHEN m.Act_Status_Machine IN ['IDLE','STOPPED','MAINTENANCE'] THEN 0.0 ELSE null END
